title: France 2005: Provence & the Alps (Clairvaux to Malaucène & the Verdon)

main_route.excerpt: John, Charles, Simon + Eve set off at 5pm for France, waved off by Thomas + Peter.
We reached __Broadhembury__ near Ashford in Kent at 10.45. ... We paid £25 to sleep on a path.

main_route.inference_reason: Diary describes a delayed crossing by 'train' (Eurotunnel Le Shuttle, a car-carrying train) after an overnight near Ashford/Folkestone in Kent; no terminal named explicitly, but the 'train' wording and Kent location point to the Channel Tunnel at Folkestone rather than a ferry.
main_route.excerpt: We reached the [?fer] train early + the train was delayed an hour. It was a bit nerve-wracking with terrorism around.
The campsite in fields outside __Laudemanche__ is light with lovely views across fields. ... We had camped east of Troyes near artificial lakes between the Seine + Aube rivers.
We reached Clairvaux Abbey, hoping to go to church, but it was all closed up until 2 pm. So we drove on though the rain + bought bread.
We drove through Grémieux in the afternoon, in a gorge with castles + a big Mediaeval market hall, but we didn't stop.
We passed a river where people were swimming + arrived at Tubins* campsite at 7.15., just as the pool closed. ... Suffocatingly hot in the night.

Bryony + William have reserved a pitch for us at Malaucene, place 23. ... We met up with William + Bryony + Swann + then asked if we could move up to the swimming pool level. __Le Bosquets__ – Simfros

We drove around __Mt. Ventoux__ (if Mt. Ventnor on Isle of Wight, Celtic "White Capes") the top is not snow, it's white limestone. We stopped off for a short walk to see an old bridge that carried the old road up the valley.
ochre & sand from walk near old ochre open-cast mines ... among "chemines des fees" - fairy chimney, some over 20ft high

We drove on to Reillanne (wheat fields + pine trees) + asked the way at a tourist info shop. The campsite (Vallee des Oiseau) that they sent us to, 4km away, was a nudist colony, so we turned the van around + set off again.
Near __Geouxlesbain__, we finally found a site, very posh, (Thorneness!) £45 a night with tennis + swimming ... Campsite Chateau Lavalle ***
We went back to the van, put down the tent, packed up + drove to Beoux. There, we went to buy croissants, pain-au-chocolate + bread
We drove on to Maia's campsite ***** "Camping au Soleil" Espar[r]on. Charles met up with Maia who has a tent on the beach with Minor + Natalie. We swam,

main_route.inference_reason: Trips start and end at home unless the diary says otherwise; the transcribed pages end at Esparron on Mon 1 Aug and never narrate the return journey, so the return leg and home arrival are inferred and uncited.

crossings.excerpt: We reached the [?fer] train early + the train was delayed an hour.
crossings.inference_reason: Outbound crossing described as a 'train' (car-carrying Eurotunnel Le Shuttle) after an overnight near Ashford/Folkestone in Kent; treated as the Channel Tunnel (Folkestone-Coquelles) rather than the default Dover-Calais ferry because the diary specifies a train, not a boat.
Return crossing not narrated in the transcribed pages; mirrored from the outbound Channel Tunnel crossing as the most likely return route. Low confidence.

stops.excerpt: We wandered round Vaison. Eve got lost. John + Simon walked up hill, very hot. ... Lovely town, musicians. ... We stopped at a veg/fruit shop on the way back to the camp for lunch.
We managed to leave at 9.10. for __Avignon__. We saw the vast square & the Palais des Papes from the outside & the church ... It was very hot so then we went back to the camp.

excursions.label: Day trip to Fontaine-de-Vaucluse & Sénanque Abbey

stops.excerpt: We left early (9.15?) to go to Fontaine-de-Vaucluse, a famous place where a river comes out of a cave, low down. We looked down into the pool, about 20m below where we stood.

stops.normalized_name: Abbaye Notre-Dame de Sénanque, Gordes, France
stops.excerpt: We drove on to the Abbaye Notre Dame de Sénanque, famous for its lavender. ... As we arrived a man was using an amazing tractor to harvest the lavender right next to the monastery.

unresolved.issue: Campsite name 'Tubins' (transcribed with a trailing asterisk) cannot be matched to a known place; it lies somewhere between the Clairvaux/Grémieux area and Malaucène on a hot day with chestnut orchards, but the exact location is uncertain — marked approximate as an overnight base rather than dropped.
unresolved.excerpt: arrived at Tubins* campsite at 7.15., just as the pool closed. ... Plantations of chestnut orchards.

unresolved.issue: Place name uncertain; a gorge town with castles and a medieval market hall passed in the afternoon between Clairvaux and Tubins. Could be Crémieu (Isère, which has a famous medieval market hall) rather than 'Grémieux'; drove through without stopping.
unresolved.excerpt: We drove through Grémieux in the afternoon, in a gorge with castles + a big Mediaeval market hall, but we didn't stop.

summary: A July-August 2005 family camping trip (Simon, Eve, John and Charles) from Birmingham via a Channel crossing by 'train' from Kent into France, then south through Clairvaux to a long fixed base at Malaucène in Provence, where they joined William and Bryony. From Malaucène they made day trips to Vaison-la-Romaine, Avignon, Fontaine-de-Vaucluse and the lavender abbey of Sénanque, and toured Mont Ventoux and the ochre 'Colorado' of Rustrel. They then moved east via Reillanne and Gréoux-les-Bains to a final lakeside base at Esparron-de-Verdon, where the diary ends with a pedalo day in the Verdon gorge on 1 August.
extraction_notes: The page bundle lists a sketch image on B_0068_L, but inspecting the scan confirms it is a mechanical diagram of a paper-making water-wheel mechanism (hammers, granite tub, cogs), NOT a geographic route sketch — so evidence-hierarchy rule 1 (route sketches outrank narrative) does not apply, and ordering follows the dated narrative. Travellers are only Simon, Eve, John and Charles; Thomas and Peter stayed behind (B_0063_R). The earlier July 8th entry on B_0063_R is a separate Malvern/Thorpeness episode at the start of the page and is not part of this trip's route. Outbound crossing: diary says they reached 'the [?fer] train' which was delayed — a car-carrying train indicates the Eurotunnel Le Shuttle (Folkestone-Coquelles), supported by the overnight near Ashford in Kent; modelled as a tunnel crossing rather than the default Dover-Calais ferry. The return journey is not narrated (pages end at Esparron on Mon 1 Aug 2005); home arrival and the return crossing are inferred with low confidence. 'Laudemanche', 'Tubins' and 'Grémieux'/'Beoux' are transcribed as-spelt and may be approximate French place names (e.g. Grémieux may be Crémieu; Beoux is most likely a Gréoux-les-Bains spelling). Note B_0072_R's date field carries an erroneous iso '1998-08-01' but the heading reads 'Monday 1st August' which in 2005 is correct as 2005-08-01.
